understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to solve each level within a limited step budget by moving the selectable piece(s) on the grid so that a selectable reaches the goal tile(s) (marked as + in the semantic grid).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in that direction, unless blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects. Some objects (blocks) are pushable: if a block is immediately adjacent in the move direction and the space beyond it allows, the selected piece can push it so both shift together; otherwise the move fails. Walls (#) act as impassable obstacles, but some boundary/wall-like tiles may have special behavior that must be discovered by attempting moves. Levels can include multiple selectable pieces: using CLICK with an object's pixel-position switches control to that selectable. The movement.*_blocked flags are only hints that something is adjacent and do not guarantee whether an attempted move will succeed.
